preview_text:
The model rates Egypt as the favorite with a 47% win probability, while New Zealand holds a 23% chance of victory. The 24-point gap between them reflects a clear but not overwhelming edge for Egypt. New Zealand enters as a substantial underdog yet retains plausible paths to three points.

The draw emerges as the single most likely outcome at 30%, suggesting the match will be competitive enough to produce a stalemate. The spread across all three outcomes is relatively balanced—no result falls below 23%—indicating genuine uncertainty. A one-goal margin in either direction appears the modal expectation given the draw's prominence.

The sportsbook consensus diverges modestly from the model. Books price Egypt's win 7 points higher (54.5% vs 47%), while compressing the draw to 26% and New Zealand to 20%. Polymarket sentiment aligns more closely with the sportsbook view on Egypt and draws, suggesting the market sees a tighter, more decisive contest than the model's forecast. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these framings.
